plant.scientific_name: Saussurea costus (Falc.) Lipsch. (Syn. Saussurea lappa (Decne.) Sch.Bip.)
plant.description: Unique History: A highly sacred and valued herb of the Himalayas, traditionally included in the group 'Deepaniya' (appetizer) and 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-relieving). Mentioned in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita for its unmatched 'Vatahara' (Vata-balancing) and 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-reliever) properties. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in the Vedas and biblical texts as a sacred incense. It is an endangered species. Physical Characteristics: A perennial herb growing in the sub-alpine and alpine regions of the Himalayas (altitude 2500m to 4000m). It features broad, heart-shaped (cordate) lower leaves and narrow, amplexicaul upper leaves. The flowers are small, bell-shaped, and usually dark-blue to black in terminal clusters. The root is thick, cylindrical, brownish-grey with a characteristic strong-musky-aromatic odor and bitter-pungent taste. It is an endangered species. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Essential Oil, Costunolide, and Dehydrocostus lactone. The presence of costunolide is crucial for its neuro-protective, anti-viral, and anti-carcinogenic action on the brain and lung cells. Strict Standardizations: The root is standardized in API based on its brownish-grey color and characteristic strong-musky-aromatic odor. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<5%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>15%), and water-soluble extractive (>20%). HPLC is used to monitor 1% to 2% costunolide in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Prominently pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas. Due to its Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-relieving) and 'Vatavyadhi' (neurological disorders) therapies, clearing the cold, heavy, and sticky qualities of Kapha through its systemic warming and penetrating property. It does not aggravate Pitta when used appropriately.

root_powder__churna_.method: 1 gram to 3 grams of fine powder taken twice daily with honey or warm water (as an Anupana) for managing pain and fevers.

root_decoction__kwatha_.method: 5g of dried root boiled in 100ml water reduced to 25ml for chronic asthma and bronchial congestion. Take twice daily before meals.

kushthadi_taila.method: Medicated oil preparation used primarily for local application on the skin and joints for pain-relief.

kushtha_ghrita.method: 5-10g of ghee processed with Kushtha; taken to treat chronic mental stress and physical weakness through its 'Rasayana' quality.

external_paste.method: Ground fresh root or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, joint pain) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection.
dosage.adults: Powder: 1-3 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Ghee: 5-10 g; Oil: 1-2 drops (internal, with precautions), divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 1g of powder daily with warm water at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: Expert Analgesic and Pain Relief Specialist (Shulaprashamana): Kushtha is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of acute and chronic pain. Its 'Tikshna' (sharp) and 'Ushna' (heating) nature provided by sesquiterpene lactones helps in clearing Vata-obstruction and providing instant relief from abdominal colic, pleurisy, and joint pain. WHO-aligned research recognizes its potent anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects.

Respiratory and Asthma Management: Historically known as 'Shwasahara' (antiasthmatic). It effectively reduces the intensity and frequency of asthma attacks and hiccups by clearing 'Kapha-avarana' (mucus-obstruction) in the respiratory channels and providing systemic warming (Ushna).

Mind and Memory Enhancement: Its 'Medhya' (intellect-promoting) quality helps in enhancing cognitive function, improving memory retention, and providing systemic calming to the nervous system through its antioxidant-rich profile. It is often combined with Vacha and Brahmi for enhanced brain recovery.

Skin and Wound Healing: Its antimicrobial and unctuous (Snigdha) properties help in treating chronic dry skin conditions and localized inflammation. The root paste applied topically promotes rapid relief from eczema and psoriasis. It is traditionally used in 'Kushthadi Taila'.

Digestive and Appetizer: Its Tikshna (sharp) and Ushna (heating) nature helps in igniting the Jatharagni (digestive fire) and cleared stagnant food from the gut. It is traditionally used to manage 'Aruchi' (tastelessness) and flatulence.

Anti-Aging and Rejuvenation: Acts as a powerful 'Rasayana' (rejuvenative). It helps in delaying age-related mental and physical decline and protecting the cells from oxidative stress through its potent antioxidant and immunity-enhancing profile.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Sesquiterpene Lactones (Costunolide, Dehydrocostus lactone), Essential Oil (approx. 1-2%), and diversas Sterols. Costunolide is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory, anti-carcinogenic, and anti-ulcer action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and incense with a history of use spanning millennia across various global traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with extreme high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity, bleeding disorders) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Avoid during pregnancy in very large therapeutic doses except under professional guidance.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin warmth or stomach dryness.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-hypertensive or heart medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
